generated_comment: I saw Meena has been putting Fair and Lovely every day, so today I bought her the big tube only. What to do, from when she was small I have worried about this colour, paavam, for girls it matters more and later for marriage people will see all this first. As a mother if I can help even a little, I should do it now itself.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it reinforces gendered beauty standards and colorism specifically for a young woman, tying her worth and future to fairness and marriageability. The surface manifestation is casual normalization of discriminatory expectations placed on daughters.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter Meena who is 20 is dark skinned like her father. Meena has started applying Fair and Lovely on her face every morning. You noticed two weeks ago and have not said anything. Today you bought her a larger tube without her asking. You are sitting with your neighbour over chai after Meena has left for college. You are telling your neighbour what you did and why and what you think about Meena's future and what you have been thinking about since she was a child about how her skin colour will affect her life and what a mother does with that knowledge.
